subject: Add Distributed Cloud Manager
commit_message:
Add Distributed Cloud Manager

This handles RESTful API CRUD operations for logical clouds, CLuster,
User Permissions, Quota, and Key Value pairs.
See: https://wiki.onap.org/x/tAiVB

Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-996
Signed-off-by: Enyinna Ochulor <enyinna.ochulor@intel.com>
Change-Id: I654a304cd682f762c02cfd92b4483d1edea63fca

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

As we spoke about:
1 - the approach that works with our curent mongo routines is to put all of your resources into the same mongo collection.
2 - switch over to use the Insert(), Find(), and Remove() mongo routines instead of Create(), Get() and Delete()
3 - The 'Key' structure you use for all of these resources (e.g. logical clouds, clusters, keyvalues, quotas, etc.) - should have a consistent and unique naming across the reousrces.  That is don't use 'name' for each one  Instead use names like "logical-cloud', 'quota', etc.

Patch Set 6:

As we spoke about:
1 - the approach that works with our curent mongo routines is to put all of your resources into the same mongo collection.
2 - switch over to use the Insert(), Find(), and Remove() mongo routines instead of Create(), Get() and Delete()
3 - The 'Key' structure you use for all of these resources (e.g. logical clouds, clusters, keyvalues, quotas, etc.) - should have a consistent and unique naming across the reousrces. 
4 - I think child resources need to have some dependency on paretns - e.g. if you query a kv-pair, then 'project' and 'logical-cloud' would be part of the query.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Regarding the handling of clusters - I believe the API needs some work.
If I'm reading the code right, there is no convenient way to find all clusters for a given logical cloud.
you would have to query the cluster-provider API for all cluster-providers and then loop thorugh that list and query for all cluster-references for each cluster-provider.
how do you know which cluster-providers are associated with a logical-cloud?
I think somehow including the cluster-provider into the cluster-reference would be a better approach.

Also, I saw a couple of the api handlers had multi-part handling code - eventhough the specific API did not need it - check all of them to ensure multi-part is only being used where needed.
